conf-proc-title-group.conf-normalized-title: Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT), 2026 4th International Conference on

conf-meta.conf-name: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)

title-group.article-title: Quantum-Inspired Intrusion Detection System Using Dual Metaheuristic Optimization and Deep Neural Analysis

aff.institution: Research Scholar \\ Department of Computer Science and Engineering \\ Karpagam Academy of Higher Education \\ Coimbatore 641021 - Tamil Nadu line \\ dinshapk5@gmail.com
Professor \\ Department of Computer Science and Engineering \\ Karpagam Academy of Higher Education \\ Coimbatore 641021- Tamil Nadu \\ santhoshrd@gmail.com

permissions.copyright-statement: 979-8-3315-9654-5 © 2026 IEEE. All rights reserved, including rights for text and data mining, and training of artificial intelligence and similar technologies. Personal use is permitted, but republication/redistribution requires IEEE permission. See https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/index.html for more information.

abstract.p: The high rate of cyber threat development poses serious threats to the traditional Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that in most cases fail to detect effectively and falsely alarms when handling high volumes of high dimensional network traffic. In order to overcome these weaknesses, this study suggests a Quantum-inspired Hybrid IDS and it combines dual metaheuristic optimization and quantum-classical Deep Learning (DL) models. The framework initially preprocesses the raw network traffic data and then, the proposed Self-Adaptive Grasshopper-Whale Optimization Algorithm (SAGWOA) is employed to select features efficiently, i.e., to reduce the number of data dimensions and maintain important data. To enhance the nonlinear learning ability, Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) is utilized which exploits quantum superposition and entanglement concepts. Besides, the proposed Quantum aided Deep Convolutional Support Vector Machine (QDC-SVM) is used for learning spatial and hierarchical patterns and performing quantum-enhanced classification. Here, the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) is used to enable the deep feature learning and Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) is employed to increase the margin separation between normal and attack traffic. Experimental analysis on benchmark datasets, such as NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 shows the proposed hybrid IDS performs superior, in terms of detection accuracy (98.64 and 98.66% respectively) and lower false alarm rates $(0.049 \%$ and 0.016 % respectively), in comparison with traditional solutions.

sec.label: I.
sec.title: Introduction
sec.p:
The interconnected systems and cloud-based services have grown exponentially, and as a result, the modern network environment has become extremely susceptible to advanced cyberattacks [1]. The conventional IDS is based on signaturebased techniques or shallow Machine Learning (ML) models is usually not able to accommodate changing attack patterns and high-dimensionality of network traffic due to large-scale networks [2]. This weakness leads to low accuracy of detection, high rates of false alarms and lack of generalization to novel threats, and it is vital to note that more intelligent and adaptive IDS frameworks should be adopted. Latest developments in the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and optimization methods have brought about the new promising tools in intrusion detection in the form of metaheuristic algorithms and DL models [3]. Generally, metaheuristic optimization algorithms are used to perform feature selection, which involves reducing dimensionality without losing important data [4]. Nevertheless, single-strategy methods cause the premature convergence or suboptimal features sets. Global exploration and local exploitation which are hybrid metaheuristic strategies used to better optimize features of complex cybersecurity datasets [5]. Simultaneously, Quantum-inspired ML (QML) models have a better learning efficiency and improved decision boundaries. By combining these models with DL models, it is possible to have a hybrid quantum-classical model that identify sophisticated attacks [6]. Thus, this study proposed a QDC-SVM which integrates dual metaheuristic optimization SAGWOA and quantumclassical DL in order to obtain high detection rates, low false alarms, and enhanced flexibility. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is proven by experimental validation on benchmark datasets, such as NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017. The main contributions are given below;
•
QDC-SVM is proposed, which is based on dual metaheuristic optimization coupled with quantumclassical DL to enhance cyberattack identification. •
SAGWOA, a feature selection algorithm based on Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm (GOA) and Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) is efficient in terms of dimensionality reduction of the data and also in terms of identifying the most significant features. •
The framework takes advantage of quantum-inspired nonlinear learning and deep feature learning which is tested on both NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 datasets with a superior detection rate and a reduced false alarm rate compared to the current methods.

sec.label: II.
sec.title: Literature Review
sec.p: In 2025, Vinotha & Eswaran [7] investigated effectiveness and accuracy of hyperparameter optimization using quantum search-enhanced bat algorithm (QS-BAT). This model combined adaptive swarm intelligence with quantum-inspired search dynamics. The WSN-DS dataset was used to optimize and assess DL architectures under various attack scenarios and the suggested model achieved 95 % accuracy and 95 % F1score. In 2025, Mohamed [8] introduced the transformational role of AI and ML in current cybersecurity, where it effectively detects and mitigates advanced cyber threats. It was a thorough study of AI-based intrusion detection, malware detection, adversarial protection, federated learning, and new technologies like quantum computing and IoT security. In 2025, Turaka and Panigrahy [9] explored a hybrid intelligent model that integrated DL, chaos theory, and quantum computing. These modules all used a graph-based classifier that has been improved with quantum attention methods. Compared to the top-performing models, this architecture achieved 4 % better classification accuracy. In 2025, Hassan et al. [10] addressed the concept of intrusion detection in vehicle networks, which was concerned with the security concerns posed by IoT-based autonomous transportation systems. They suggested a hybrid strategy of using correlation-based feature selection and PCA to reduce the size and use dense neural network classification. In 2025, Turaka and Panigrahy [11] incorporated chaotic maps, Chaotic Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization (CAPSO) for preventing premature convergence in IDS. Compared to normal PSO, it achieved 40 % feature reduction, and 98 % accuracy. Besides, quantum aided Genetic Algorithm (GA) achieved 50% feature reduction, 2% higher accuracy.

sec.label: A.
sec.title: Problem Statement
sec.p: In spite of serious development of AI-, ML-, and quantumbased security systems, there are still several important issues to consider in the real-world context of intrusion detection and analysis of network traffic. Current QML methods are not thoroughly tested on large-scale, real-world settings and are usually limited in terms of interpretability and sensitivity to architectural and noise related conditions. Though powerful, AI-based cybersecurity systems are susceptible to adversarial attacks, have problems with scalability, and decentralized systems have privacy concerns. Moreover, the latest intrusion detection frameworks in vehicular and IoT networks cannot balance the accuracy of detection and low computation and model size. Such gaps reveal the necessity of an effective, explainable, and scalable hybrid IDS that integrates effective feature optimization with recent quantum-inspired and deep learning solutions to be used in the real world.

sec.label: III.
sec.title: Proposed Methodology
sec.p:
The proposed quantum-inspired IDS initially processes and normalizes the data on the network and then uses the GOA to select the initial features. They then use a hybrid SAGWOA strategy to trade-off between global exploration and local exploitation and determine the best feature subset. Quantuminspired models, such as VQC and QSVM along with CNN which are used to develop the proposed QDC-SVM to encode and analyze selected features and provide greater performance on IDS. On benchmark datasets, the framework is tested and it is found to have better detection accuracy and lower false alarms. Fig. 1 depicts the overall architecture of proposed model.
Fig. 1.
Overall architecture of proposed model

sec.label: A.
sec.title: Data Preprocessing and Normalization
sec.p: Normalization of data is carried out through Z-score normalization [13], that normalizes each feature by the mean and standard deviation. This is done to make the values of the features be in a common scale that enhances numerical stability and makes the model training efficient is given in Eq.

sec.label: B.
sec.title: Feature Selection Using Proposed Sagwoa
sec.p: The proposed SAGWOA combines the GOA to explore the entire world and the WOA [15] to exploit the small features to finesse the feature subset selection process. This mixed method uses the capability of GOA to search the space in a broad manner and the exploitation power of WOA to narrow in on the best solution.

A linearly decreases from 2 to 0 over iterations to reduce exploration and increase exploitation is given in Eq.~(\ref{eqn14}), where, $\vec{r}$ is a random vector with components uniformly distributed in [0], [1], and $T_{\text {max }}$ is the maximum number of iterations.

sec.label: C.
sec.title: Quantum Feature Encoding Using Vqc

sec.label: D.
sec.title: Ids Modeling Using Proposed Qdc-Cnn
sec.p:
Fig. 2 delivers the architecture of proposed QDC-SVM.
Fig. 2
Architecture of proposed qdc-svm

sec.label: IV.
sec.title: Results and Discussion
sec.p: The experimental analysis of the suggested QDC-SVM was performed to evaluate its usefulness in the comparison with classical and existing models of quantum-aided intrusion detection. Standard benchmark datasets, i.e. NSL-KDD [19] and CICIDS2017 [20], were subjected to experiments in terms of various performance measures, such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, detection rate, and false-positive rate (FPR). The proposed model was contrasted with the classical approaches like SVM, CNN [18], and standalone quantum classifiers like VQC [16] and QSVM [17]. Findings show that QDC-SVM is a better detection method than these methods, in regards to detection accuracy and the convergence speed. In addition, the hybridization of dual metaheuristic optimization with deep and quantum learning allows better flexibility to known and zero-day cyberattacks.

sec.title: Performance of Proposed Qdc-Svm over Traditional Models
sec.p: Table I shows the results of performance comparison of various intrusion detection models on the NSL-KDD data.

Classical SVM and CNN models are reasonable in terms of detection; though their accuracy and recall are limited in detecting complicated attack patterns. VQC and QSVM are quantum-based models that demonstrate a significant improvement, which proves the efficiency of quantum learning to improve decision boundaries. Compared to all the baseline methods, the proposed QDC-SVM is significantly more accurate, precise, and recall and F1-score, and it has the lowest false-positive rate, which proves its higher detection ability and strength. Table II shows the relative performance of different models of intrusion detection on the CICIDS2017 dataset. Conventional SVM and CNN methods are less precise in detection and more false-positive because of the complex and varied nature of traffic patterns in the dataset. Quantuminspired learning has the benefit of quantum-based classifiers, such as VQC and QSVM, which are more accurate and with lower FPR. The proposed QDC-SVM is the most effective in general performance, demonstrating the most accurate, most precise, most recalling, and the most F1-score, and the lowest false-positive rate, validating the effectiveness and scalability of the proposed model to the real-world intrusion detection situation.
Table I. Performance comparison of ids models on nslkdd dataset
Model Accuracy (%) Precision (%) Recall (%) F1Score (%) FPR
SVM 91.82 90.35 89.64 89.98 0.071
CNN 94.56 93.92 93.17 93.54 0.054
VQC 95.12 94.38 93.96 94.17 0.049
QSVM 95.73 94.95 94.32 94.63 0.043
Proposed QDCSVM 98.64 98.32 98.18 98.25 0.017
Table II. Performance comparison of ids models on cicids2017 dataset
Model Accuracy (%) Precision (%) Recall (%) F1Score (%) FPR
SVM 90.47 89.83 88.92 89.37 0.085
CNN 93.95 93.41 92.98 93.19 0.058
VQC 95.23 94.88 94.32 94.6 0.045
QSVM 95.84 95.32 94.97 95.14 0.039
Proposed QDCSVM 98.66 98.41 98.19 98.3 0.016
Fig. 3 and 4 states the performance of proposed model on both NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 datasets, where the propsoed QDC-SVM attained enhanced performance all other traditional models which highlights the efficacy of the developed model in IDS.
Fig 3.
Proposed model performance on nsl-kdd dataset
Fig 4.
Proposed model performance on cicids2017 dataset

sec.label: V.
sec.title: Conclusion
sec.p: This study brought forth a Quantum-Inspired Hybrid IDS, which is a Quantum-aided Deep Convolutional Support Vector Machine (QDC-SVM) with a combination of two metaheuristic feature optimization and quantum-classical deep learning. The hybrid SAGWOA feature selection was effective to decrease the dimensionality, as well as maintain the discriminative information, leading to higher detection accuracy and lower false alarms. Experimental experiments with NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 datasets have proven that the proposed framework is superior to classical and existing quantum-based IDS models. CNN, QSVM, and quantum feature encoding combination increased the learning ability and adaptation to complicated attack patterns. The next generation of work may be concerned with applying the framework to a real quantum device, scaling it to online and distributed IDS deployments, and further develop transformer-based quantum-deep architecture as an approach to real-time detection of cyber threats.
